title: Wild Swans
content:
I looked in my heart while the wild swans went over.
             And what did I see I had not seen before?
             Only a question less or a question more;
             Nothing to match the flight of wild birds flying.
             Tiresome heart, forever living and dying,
             House without air, I leave you and lock your door.
             Wild swans, come over the town, come over
             The town again, trailing your legs and crying!
meaning: About: Edna St. Vincent Millay was an American lyrical poet and playwright and the first woman to receive the Pulitzer Prize for Poetry. She was also known for her unconventional, bohemian lifestyle and her many love affairs. She used the pseudonym Nancy Boyd for her prose work.
